title: Multiply by 2
description: Multiply 2 times a number less than or equal to 10.
topic_path: Module 1: Properties of multiplication and division and solving problems with units of 2–5 and 10
Topic C: Multiplication using units of 2 and 3

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to help solve $2 \times 7$.* 

$2 \times 7 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The number line shows $\blueD{2}$ equal jumps of $\maroonD{7}$ units.


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 14 with tick marks at every 1 unit. An arrow begins at the 0 tick mark and moves right on the number line, pointing to the labeled tick marks at 7 and 14.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply.**  
*Hint: Use the array below to help solve $6 \times 2$.*   

$6 \times 2 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The array shows $\blueD6$ rows with $\purpleC2$ squares in each row.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{6} \times \purpleC{2} = \underbrace{\purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC2 +\purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC2}_{\blueD{6}\purpleC{\text{ twos}}} = \greenD{12}$
$\blueD6 \times \purpleC2 =\greenD{12}$

options.alt: An array of squares arranged in 6 rows of 2 squares each.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply.**  
*Hint: Use the array below to help solve $10 \times 2$.*   

$10 \times 2 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The array shows $\blueD{10}$ rows with $\purpleC2$ squares in each row.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{10} \times \purpleC{2} = \underbrace{\purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC2 +\purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC2 +\purpleC{2} + \purpleC{2} + \purpleC2}_{\blueD{10}\purpleC{\text{ twos}}} = \greenD{20}$
$\blueD{10} \times \purpleC2 =\greenD{20}$

options.alt: An array of squares arranged in 10 rows of 2 squares each.
